chunks.text: Who wants to be a Google Games Champion? - EECS at Berkeley Come on down, Berkeley students! Do you want to be a contestant at this years Google Games? Is your team ready to challenge yourselves, win prizes and have fun? Well, we invite you to register for The Google Games Bay Area taking place on Saturday, April 14th . Survey says that contestants will compete against other students from UC Berkeley and Stanford in challenges inspired by iconic game-shows, including Family Feud, Jeopardy and more. All mini games and challenges will test your brains and creativity to the limit, as you play for the title of The Google Games Champions. It’s a day of fun (and games!) as teams of five go head to head in many different challenges, including: fun puzzle competitions, interactive word association

head in many different challenges, including: fun puzzle competitions, interactive word association, and some light coding portions. Here’s what you’ll need to do by Friday, March 30th : Assemble a team of five freshman, sophomore, and or/junior students from your school (having a CS background is strongly recommended). Pick a team name and register here (each team member must register individually). Check out our website for more information and pictures from previous years! Review the details and FAQs below The Google Games Bay Area Date: Saturday, April 14th Time: 10:00am to approximately 5:00pm Location: Google Building MP7, Sunnyvale CA 94089 *Don’t forget to register here* .
